properties.name: Cordell Bank National Marine Sanctuary
properties.manager: NOAA Office of National Marine Sanctuaries
properties.description: Offshore California sanctuary protecting a submerged granite bank and its associated deep-sea and pelagic ecosystem.

properties.name: Channel Islands National Marine Sanctuary
properties.manager: NOAA Office of National Marine Sanctuaries
properties.description: Waters surrounding five of the Channel Islands off Southern California; a biologically diverse transition zone between cold and warm Pacific currents.

properties.name: Flower Garden Banks National Marine Sanctuary
properties.manager: NOAA Office of National Marine Sanctuaries
properties.description: Coral reefs and banks on the Texas–Louisiana outer continental shelf in the Gulf of Mexico.

properties.name: Florida Keys National Marine Sanctuary
properties.manager: NOAA Office of National Marine Sanctuaries
properties.description: Third-largest barrier reef ecosystem in the world, encompassing the Florida Keys coral reef tract.

properties.name: Gray's Reef National Marine Sanctuary
properties.manager: NOAA Office of National Marine Sanctuaries
properties.description: Live-bottom reef 17 nmi off Sapelo Island, GA — one of the largest nearshore live-bottom reefs in the southeastern United States.

properties.name: Hawaiian Islands Humpback Whale National Marine Sanctuary
properties.manager: NOAA Office of National Marine Sanctuaries (co-managed with the State of Hawaiʻi)
properties.description: Protects the primary wintering, mating, calving, and nursing grounds for the North Pacific humpback whale population.

properties.name: Monterey Bay National Marine Sanctuary
properties.manager: NOAA Office of National Marine Sanctuaries
properties.description: The nation’s largest contiguous NMS; kelp forests, open ocean, and the Monterey Submarine Canyon.

properties.name: Mallows Bay–Potomac River National Marine Sanctuary
properties.manager: NOAA Office of National Marine Sanctuaries
properties.description: Freshwater tidal sanctuary centered on the "Ghost Fleet of Mallows Bay" — nearly 100 WWI-era wooden steamship wrecks.

properties.name: National Marine Sanctuary of American Samoa
properties.manager: NOAA Office of National Marine Sanctuaries (co-managed with American Samoa)
properties.description: Six protected management areas across the Samoan archipelago, including Fagatele Bay and Rose Atoll.

properties.name: Olympic Coast National Marine Sanctuary
properties.manager: NOAA Office of National Marine Sanctuaries (co-managed with the Makah, Quileute, Hoh, and Quinault tribes)
properties.description: 3,188 sq mi along 135 mi of Washington’s outer coast — temperate rain-forest shoreline and productive continental shelf habitats.

properties.name: Papahānaumokuākea Marine National Monument
properties.manager: NOAA, USFWS, State of Hawaiʻi, and the Office of Hawaiian Affairs (co-trustees)
properties.description: One of the largest marine protected areas on Earth. UNESCO World Heritage Site. Expanded in 2016 to 582,578 sq mi.

properties.name: Stellwagen Bank National Marine Sanctuary
properties.manager: NOAA Office of National Marine Sanctuaries
properties.description: Glacially-deposited underwater plateau at the mouth of Massachusetts Bay; critical feeding ground for endangered North Atlantic humpback, fin, and right whales.

properties.name: Thunder Bay National Marine Sanctuary
properties.manager: NOAA Office of National Marine Sanctuaries (co-managed with the State of Michigan)
properties.description: Protects ≈200 known shipwrecks in northwestern Lake Huron; the only freshwater NMS.
